zh.lifeSummary: 當Tristram Thomas於1522年出生在英國時，他的父親Nicolas Tristram de Court Thomas26歲，母親Lady Elizabeth Rose Griffin20歲。他於1539年與Elizabeth Anne Coursey Madison在英國肯特設維寧結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1558年在英國英格蘭肯特被列為rector of st nicolas church。他埋葬在英國英格蘭肯特設維寧。

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, French, Walloon, Breton, German, Dutch, Flemish, Danish, Greek, West Indian (mainly Haiti and Jamaica), and African (mainly Tanzania and Nigeria): from the personal name Thomas, of Biblical (New Testament) origin, from Aramaic t’ōm’a, a byname meaning ‘twin’. It was borne by one of the disciples of Christ, known for his scepticism about Christ's resurrection (John 20:24–29). The Th- spelling is organic, the initial letter of the name in the Greek New Testament being a theta. The English pronunciation as t rather than th- is the result of French influence from an early date. In Britain, the surname is widely distributed throughout the country, but especially common in Wales and Cornwall. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages (e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean or Arabic Toma and Tuma , Albanian Toma and Thoma , and Slavic surnames listed in 3 below), and their patronymics and other derivatives (e.g. Polish Tomaszewski and Slovenian Tomažič; see Tomazic ). In France, this surname is most common in the Vosges and Brittany. The name Thomas is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Machan , Mammen , and Oommen ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Native American (e.g. Navajo): adoption of the English personal name Thomas (see 1 above) as a surname.

Germanized or Americanized form of Polish Tomas , Tomasz, and Tomaś, Sorbian Tomaš (see also 4 below), Croatian Tomaš and Tomas , Slovenian Tomaš and Tomaž, Czech and Slovak Tomáš, all meaning ‘Thomas’.

variants.name: Maas
meanings: Dutch, Flemish, and North German: from a short form of the personal name   Thomas  .

variants.name: Mashak
meanings: Americanized form of Polish Maszak: patronymic from a short form of the personal name Tomasz (see Thomas ) or possibly of a personal name beginning with Ma-. The surname Maszak is very rare in Poland.
In some cases also an Americanized form of Czech Mašek (see Masek ). Compare Mashek .

variants.name: Maschke
meanings: German: from a Slavic-influenced (see below) pet form of the personal names   Thomas   (compare   Maas  ) or   Matthias  .
Germanized form of Sorbian Mašk, Maška, and Maško: from a pet form of the personal name Domaš or Tomaš (see Thomas 3, 4), or of any name beginning with Ma-.
Germanized form of Czech Mašek (see Masek ) and Polish Maszek, both cognates of 2 above.

variants.name: Toma
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Kamal, Nabil, Salem, Kamil, Safa, Samir, Adil, Adnan, Aziz, Bahjat, Basem, Bashar.  Japanese Hiroshi, Takeo, Hideo, Isako, Izumi, Junichi, Kenji, Kiyoko, Masakazu, Rikuo, Sachi, Shigeo.
Romanian and Croatian: from the personal name Toma, an equivalent of Thomas . Compare Tomas .

Italian, French, Polish, Slovak, and Hungarian: from a short, pet, or dialect form of a personal name equivalent to Thomas (Italian Tommaso, French Thomas, Polish Tomasz, Slovak Tomáš, Hungarian Tamás).

variants.name: Mask
meanings: English: unexplained.
German: from a pet form of the personal name   Thomas  .

variants.name: Masino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Ciro, Larraine.
Italian: from a short form of Tommasino, a pet form of thepersonal name Tommaso (see Thomas ).

variants.name: Mascio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Carlo, Gino, Luigi, Paolo, Salvatore.
Italian:
(southern): from a variant of Maso, a short form of the personal name Tommaso (see Thomas ). Alternatively, perhaps from an Italianized form of the Albanian equivalent Mashi.

variants.name: Masel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt.
German: from a pet form of   Thomas  .

variants.name: Masi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Carmine, Mario, Vito, Carlo, Antonio, Alfonso, Armando, Filomena, Gerardo, Giovanni, Salvatore, Alfonse, Alfonsina, Amato, Anello, Canio, Emilio.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of Maso, a short form of the personal name Tommaso (see Thomas ).

variants.name: Masiello
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carlo, Rocco, Angelo, Carmine, Franco, Salvatore, Antonio, Carmino, Domenic, Domenica, Francesco, Mauro.
Italian (mainly Naples): from a pet form of the personal name Tommaso (see Thomas ).

nameMeanings.name: Tristram
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of Tristan . Both forms of the name occur in medieval and later versions of the legend. In Laurence Sterne's comic novel Tristram Shandy ( 1759–67 ), the name is bestowed on the narrator through a misunderstanding and is regarded by his father as a great misfortune. Since the name originally intended for him was Trismegistus, the degree of misfortune may be taken as somewhat exaggerated.

variants.name: Tristam
meanings: Variant of Tristan . Both forms of the name occur in medieval and later versions of the legend. In Laurence Sterne's comic novel Tristram Shandy ( 1759–67 ), the name is bestowed on the narrator through a misunderstanding and is regarded by his father as a great misfortune. Since the name originally intended for him was Trismegistus, the degree of misfortune may be taken as somewhat exaggerated.

variants.name: Trystram
meanings: Variant of Tristan . Both forms of the name occur in medieval and later versions of the legend. In Laurence Sterne's comic novel Tristram Shandy ( 1759–67 ), the name is bestowed on the narrator through a misunderstanding and is regarded by his father as a great misfortune. Since the name originally intended for him was Trismegistus, the degree of misfortune may be taken as somewhat exaggerated.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Tristram Thomas was born in 1522, in England, his father, Nicolas Tristram de Court Thomas, was 26 and his mother, Lady Elizabeth Rose Griffin, was 20. He married Elizabeth Anne Coursey Madison in 1539, in Chevening, Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. In 1558, at the age of 36, his occupation is listed as rector of st nicolas church in Kent, England, United Kingdom. He was buried in Chevening, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Elizabeth Anne Coursey Madison en 1539, à Chevening, Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. 1558, als er 36 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als rector of st nicolas church in Kent, England, United Kingdom aufgeführt. Lui sposato Elizabeth Anne Coursey Madison nel 1539, nel Chevening, Kent, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Tristram Thomasが1522年にイギリスで生まれた時，父Nicolas Tristram de Court Thomasは26歳，母Lady Elizabeth Rose Griffinは20歳であった。1539年にイギリスケントチェベニングでElizabeth Anne Coursey Madisonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。1558年，36歳の時，イギリスイギリスケントでの職業はrector of st nicolas churchと記録されている。彼はイギリスイギリスケントチェベニングにて埋葬された。

ko.St Mary Magdalen, Bermondsey, Surrey, En: 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 캉르버이 캉르버이 스트 메리 막그다렌
ko.lifeSummary: Tristram Thomas(이)가 1522년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Nicolas Tristram de Court Thomas(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Elizabeth Rose Griffin(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1539년 영국 켄트 처베닝에서 Elizabeth Anne Coursey Madison(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1558년, 36세의 나이로 그의 직업은 영국 잉글랜드 켄트에서 rector of st nicolas church이었다. 그는 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 처베닝에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Anne Coursey Madison em 1539, em Chevening, Kent, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. ru.St Mary Magdalen, Bermondsey, Surrey, En: кантирбури ст мэри магдеилен, кантирбури, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Tristram Thomas родился в 1522 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени Nicolas Tristram de Court Thomas было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Elizabeth Rose Griffin, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Anne Coursey Madison в 1539 г. в местности чевенинг, кэнт, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. В 1558 году, в возрасте 36 лет,  он работал по профессии rector of st nicolas church в местности кэнт, Англия, Великобритания. Он был похоронен в местности чевенинг, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Tristram Thomas nació en 1522, en Inglaterra, su papá, Nicolas Tristram de Court Thomas, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Lady Elizabeth Rose Griffin, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Anne Coursey Madison en 1539, en Chevening, Kent, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. En 1558, a la edad de 36 años, su ocupación registrada era rector of st nicolas church en Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fue sepultado en Chevening, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
